hero.thesis: Launch a niche podcast featuring solo founder interviews, targeting niche communities. Monetize through sponsorships and listener support.
hero.marketSize: Podcast market projected to reach $4 billion by 2024 with niche podcasts growing rapidly.
hero.payingEvidence: Podcasts like 'How I Built This' and 'The Indie Hackers Podcast' have proven sponsorship models.

actions.action: Research 5 niche markets with growing communities using subreddits and LinkedIn groups.
Select your niche and define your target audience persona.
Draft initial podcast format and episode structure in Notion.
Register a domain related to your podcast's niche using Namecheap.
Set up a basic podcast website using Webflow with a subscription form.
Sign up for Buzzsprout to host your podcast episodes.
List 20 potential solo founders to interview using LinkedIn search.
Create a content calendar for the next 8 episodes in Notion.
Outline your first episode script and interview questions.
Send interview requests to 10 potential guests using Apollo.io.
Record your first episode using Zoom and Audacity for editing.
Schedule episode release on Buzzsprout and promote on Twitter.
actions.success: Episode scheduled and promotion tweet posted.

ninetyDay.title: Launch and Promote

actions.action: Launch your first episode on Buzzsprout and share on niche Reddit communities.
Engage with comments and feedback from listeners.
Send a thank you email to guests and ask for referrals.

ninetyDay.metric: Episode launch and initial listener engagement.

actions.action: Research 10 potential sponsors aligned with your niche on LinkedIn and Crunchbase.
Draft a sponsorship proposal template and customize for 3 potential sponsors.
Follow up with sponsors and track responses in Notion.
Collaborate with 3 other podcasters in your niche for cross-promotion.
Run a giveaway on Twitter to increase engagement and subscribers.
Analyze engagement metrics and adjust content strategy accordingly.
Invest in a quality USB microphone and headphones within $50 budget.
Learn basic audio editing techniques on Coursera.
Edit two episodes to enhance sound quality and clarity.

ninetyDay.title: Advanced Monetization Strategies

actions.action: Explore Patreon setup for listener support and bonus content.
Design a tiered sponsorship package in Notion.
Reach out to 5 new sponsors with updated packages.

ninetyDay.metric: Patreon launched and new sponsor pitches.

actions.action: Create a Discord server for listener interactions and community building.
Host a live Q&A session with a past guest on Discord.
Promote the Discord community on all podcast episodes.
Analyze episode performance metrics on Buzzsprout.
Optimize marketing channels based on highest engagement sources.
Plan content updates based on listener feedback and analytics.
Review financial performance and sponsorship deals.
Identify tasks to outsource and potential tools for automation.
Draft a 6-month growth strategy document.

toolStack.name: Apollo.io
toolStack.why: Find and reach out to podcast guests and sponsors.

toolStack.name: Notion
toolStack.why: Organize episode scripts, guest outreach, and sponsorship tracking.

firstTenCustomers.script:
Subject: [Name], Let's Share Your Founder Journey on My Podcast

Hi [Name],

I'm [Your Name], and I host a podcast that dives into the stories of solo founders like yourself. Our audience is eager to hear authentic journeys and insights from niche creators. I'd love to feature your story in a 30-minute episode. We can record via Zoom at your convenience. Let me know if you're interested!

Best,
[Your Name]
firstTenCustomers.cadence: Send 10 emails per week, follow up after 5 days.

pricing.starter: $150 per episode for small businesses and startups.
pricing.premium: $500 per episode for established brands and high-profile founders.
pricing.raisingRules: Raise prices after reaching 1,000 downloads per episode.

failureModes: Trap: Lack of niche focus leading to diluted content.
Trap: Poor audio quality driving listener drop-off.
Trap: Ineffective sponsor outreach yielding no deals.
Trap: Over-reliance on a single marketing channel.
Trap: Failure to reinvest in production quality.

setup.llc: Use Stripe Atlas to register an LLC for $500.
setup.payments: Set up payments through Stripe for sponsor invoicing.
setup.contracts: Use HelloSign for digital signature of sponsorship agreements.
setup.insurance: Consider podcast liability insurance once revenue exceeds $1,000/month.

scaling: As you grow, consider outsourcing editing to a freelancer from platforms like Lovable. Automate episode scheduling and social media posts using n8n to free up your time for content creation and sponsorship development. Build a small team by hiring a community manager to engage listeners and a marketing specialist to expand your reach. This structure allows you to focus on host duties and guest relationships, ensuring steady quality and growth.
